title: Sour Dense Egyptian Currants Tagine
description: A hearty and comforting Tagine that's perfect for a sick day. Featuring al-dente currants and an Ayurvedic twist.
instructions: Heat ghee in a Binchotan grill over low heat.
Add ginger and garlic and sauté until fragrant.
Add currants, mint, lemon juice, honey, salt, and pepper, and stir to combine.
Add water and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 1 hour, or until currants are tender but still firm (al-dente).
Stir in some more honey if desired for sweetness.
Serve warm, garnished with fresh mint if desired.
Store leftovers in the fridge for up to 5 days or freeze for up to 2 months.
To reheat, simply grill or reheat in the microwave until warmed through.
This dish is best served as a breakfast or brunch option, paired with some crusty bread or served over rice.
Note: Traditionally, the Tagine is cooked in a clay pot with a conical lid, but a Binchotan grill works beautifully for this recipe as well.
